response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with web, mobile, and some embedded background, but the role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, performance analysis of deep-learning workloads, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear on the resume. Education is a Bachelor's in CS without the 8+ years in a relevant hardware/performance domain, and there is no evidence of SIMD/MIMD, GPU architecture, or DNN optimization work. Misalignment is too large to justify an interview for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) as a senior/staff software engineer across diverse stacks
Some low-latency systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic)
Brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI accelerator experience
No demonstrated background in deep learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture
Education and experience profile is web/full-stack focused, not hardware/performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and AI accelerator expertise
No deep-learning workload or GPU architecture experience
Some low-latency systems and C/C++ background
